props.title: QA Control
props.subtitle: Pick a test user, then choose how to start.
props.loginDesc: Sign in and go to where this user left off.
props.restartDesc: Delete this user data, keep the account, run onboarding from the top.
props.reonboardDesc: Go through onboarding again with the data already saved.
props.resetDesc: Wipe this user data, keep the account. No onboarding.

meta.spokenContent: Hey, I might have startled you. You're probably not used to an app just talking to you. I'm your AI coach here at your service at Guided Growth, and I'm excited to help you improve things in your life that are important to you. So let's get you in, and we'll get started.

mp3Assets.transcript: Hey, I might have startled you. You're probably not used to an app just talking to you. I'm your AI coach here at your service at Guided Growth, and I'm excited to help you improve things in your life that are important to you. So let's get you in, and we'll get started.

narration.say: Hey, I might have startled you. You're probably not used to an app just talking to you. I'm your AI coach here at your service at Guided Growth, and I'm excited to help you improve things in your life that are important to you. So let's get you in, and we'll get started.

meta.expectedResponse: Taps Apple, Google, or email sign-in

props.heading: Allow your microphone
props.sub: So you can talk with your coach out loud.
props.coachLine: I'd love to actually talk with you. If you let me use your mic, you can just speak.

meta.spokenContent: I'd love to actually talk with you. If you let me use your mic, you can just speak.

mp3Assets.label: mic_permission_1
mp3Assets.transcript: I'd love to actually talk with you. If you let me use your mic, you can just speak.

beats.name: Profile (age + gender)
beats.context:
BEAT: Profile setup.

Name, age, and gender are all required. Gender can be Other, but it has to be answered. No skipping, no soft fields here. We don't ask for referral (where they heard about us) right now. Keep that field parked for later, do not collect it in this beat yet.

We usually already have the user's name from their Google or Apple sign-in, so open by welcoming them by name and confirm it's what they want to be called, then ask age and gender. If we don't have a name (for example an email sign-up), ask for the name first. Use the name warmly, once. Accept voice or taps interchangeably, fill the fields as answers come in, and never re-ask something you already have. This is the first moment the coach knows who they are, so keep it light, not like a form.

PATHS

Path 1 (full voice, talk and listen): If we have the name, open with it ("Hey Sarah, good to have you. Want me to call you Sarah?"), then ask age and gender. The user can answer in one go and the fields fill live. Run the pronunciation check only when the name is uncommon: a short "did I say that right?", cap at 2 tries, then accept their spelling. If the user corrects the name at any point, fix it on the spot. If we don't have a name, ask for it first. Ask only for fields still missing.

Path 2 (half voice): If the coach speaks and the mic is off, say the welcome and the age and gender ask once, and let the user fill the fields by tapping. A short spoken name-back is fine, no pronunciation loop. If the mic is on and the coach is in text, let the user speak their answers and confirm them in writing.

Path 3 (typing only): Show the welcome and the fields, with the name prefilled if we have it. The user confirms or edits the name and taps age and gender. Confirm warmly in text, using the name once. No spoken name read-back, no pronunciation step.

All three fields required before continuing, on every path. Same fields, same save, same next step. Only the delivery and the way the user answers change.

props.greeting: Good to meet you, {name}. Two quick things so I can tailor this to you.

meta.spokenContent: Good to meet you, {name}. Two quick things so I can tailor this to you.
meta.expectedResponse: Says or taps age and gender

mp3Assets.label: onboard_01_form_1
mp3Assets.transcript: How old are you?

mp3Assets.label: onboard_01_form_2
mp3Assets.transcript: What's your gender?

beats.name: Path choice
beats.context:
BEAT: Experience fork.

Collect whether the user is new to habit tracking or already has habits they want to bring in. Route first-timers, people who tried and dropped off, or people who want guidance to beginner. Route users with an existing habit list or tracking system to advanced. If unclear, ask one short clarifying question.

PATHS

Same question and same routing on every path. Only the answer changes.
Path 1 (full voice): Ask it conversationally, the user answers by talking, you route.
Path 2 (half voice): Speak the question once and let them tap new vs experienced, or let them say it and confirm in text.
Path 3 (typing): Show the question and two choices, the user taps one.

props.coachLine: For the next part of the process, I'd like to know:

meta.spokenContent: For the next part of the process, I'd like to know:
meta.expectedResponse: New, or I already track habits

mp3Assets.label: onboard_fork_form_1
mp3Assets.transcript: For the next part of the process, I'd like to know:

mp3Assets.label: fork_question
mp3Assets.transcript: Do you already track habits or is this new to you?

perElement.line: Do you already track habits or is this new to you?

narration.say: For the next part of the process, I'd like to know:
Do you already track habits or is this new to you?

beats.name: Category tiles
beats.context:
BEAT: Focus area.

Collect one focus category. Ask what feels most worth improving now. If the user names several, help them choose the one that feels most urgent. Keep the response specific to their chosen category. Do not praise, advise, or let the beat collect multiple categories.

PATHS

Same content on every path. One category only. The category-specific response stays the same words, spoken or shown.
Path 1 (full voice): Ask what feels most worth improving, the user says it, you respond with the line specific to their category.
Path 2 (half voice): Speak the ask once and let them tap a category tile, or let them say it and confirm in text.
Path 3 (typing): Show the categories as tiles, the user taps one, the specific response is text.

props.coachLine: Let's choose one area of your life that you'd like to improve on. Here are our recommended categories.

meta.spokenContent: Let's choose one area of your life that you'd like to improve on. Here are our recommended categories.

mp3Assets.label: onboard_beginner_01_1
mp3Assets.transcript: Let's choose one area of your life that you'd like to improve on. Here are our recommended categories.

mp3Assets.label: create_your_own
mp3Assets.transcript: Or you can create your own.

perElement.line: Or you can create your own.

narration.say: Let's choose one area of your life that you'd like to improve on. Here are our recommended categories.
Or you can create your own.

beats.name: Subcategory picker
beats.context:
BEAT: Goal narrowing.

Collect one or two specific goals inside the chosen category. Offer only valid goals from that category, using the provided option names exactly. If they speak generally, map to the closest valid goal or ask one clarifying question. Do not invent, rename, or paraphrase goal labels.

PATHS

Same content on every path. One or two goals, only the valid labels for the chosen category.
Path 1 (full voice): Offer two or three examples out loud, the user picks or describes, you play the goal-specific response.
Path 2 (half voice): Speak the options once and let them tap, or let them speak and confirm in text.
Path 3 (typing): Show the goal options as cards plus a freeform field, the user taps or types.

props.coachLine: So within that, which goals would you like to start with? Pick one or two.

meta.spokenContent: So within that, which goals would you like to start with? Pick one or two.

mp3Assets.label: onboard_beginner_02_1
mp3Assets.transcript: So within that, which goals would you like to start with? Pick one or two.

props.coachLine: Tell me the goal you want to add, and I'll set it up.

meta.spokenContent: Tell me the goal you want to add, and I'll set it up.

mp3Assets.label: onboard_beginner_02_custom_1
mp3Assets.transcript: Tell me the goal you want to add, and I'll set it up.

beats.name: Habit picker
beats.context:
BEAT: Habit selection.

Collect one to three habits tied to the chosen goals. Encourage doable, not heroic. Use the provided habit options when available, and accept a custom habit name if they offer one. Require at least one habit before continuing. If they choose more than three, help them narrow without making it feel like failure.

PATHS

Same content on every path. At least one habit, one to three total, custom allowed.
Path 1 (full voice): Present the habits, the user picks or names a custom one out loud, you set it up.
Path 2 (half voice): Speak the options once and let them tap, or let them speak and confirm in text.
Path 3 (typing): Show the habit cards plus a create-your-own option, the user taps or types.

props.coachLine: Pick one or two habits that feel doable. One habit that you actually keep is much better than a list of five that you don't keep. Create your own if nothing here fits.

meta.spokenContent: Pick one or two habits that feel doable. One habit that you actually keep is much better than a list of five that you don't keep. Create your own if nothing here fits.
meta.expectedResponse: Picks habits: one per goal if two goals, one or two if one goal

props.coachLine: I'd like to invite you into a coaching process together. And it's built on a few components we'll go through on the way in. It's built light. I believe less is more, especially in the beginning of a process.

Whether you've never done something like this before or you already track a lot, it is built for you. I'll explain each part as we go. This is the first part, a quick state check-in. And I'd like you to do it right now.
How's your sleep?
How's your mood?
How's your energy?
How's your stress?

props.coachLine: Part of the coaching process is doing this each day. It gives us two things. First, it's a real quick check-in on how your state is, which is valuable, and people don't usually do it enough. And second, over time it lets us see the connection between your behavior and your state. So when would you like to do this each morning? I recommend 15 minutes after you wake up.
props.coachLine2: Every single day is great. But doing weekdays consistently is better than every day inconsistently. So that's what I recommend to start. But you're welcome to add the weekend as well.

perElement.line: Weekdays, weekends, or your own days?

beats.name: The Weekly day setup
beats.context:
BEAT: The Weekly day.

Set up The Weekly, the once-a-week session where you and the user look back over the whole week and plan the next one together. Your opener carries the pitch and is spoken as written. After it, recommend the day out loud, then let them choose.

Recommend the evening before their work week starts:
- If you are speaking Hebrew, that day is מוצ״ש (Saturday night). Say מוצ״ש, the natural Israeli word for it, not the formal motzaei Shabbat.
- Otherwise, recommend Sunday night.
Frame it as the evening before their week gets going, then ask if that works or if another day fits them better. The card already has that day preselected to match, so they can keep it or pick another.

Capture the day with submit_weekly_config, then move on.

DO NOT:
- Read the seven days out loud. The card shows them.
- Oversell. Opener, the day, let them choose, move on.
- Let it be skipped silently. If they truly will not pick, set the recommended day and tell them they can change it later.

props.coachLine: Once a week, we'll take the whole week and look at it, then plan the next one. And the insights we come up with together get clearer every week, as I get to know you.

meta.spokenContent: Once a week, we'll take the whole week and look at it, then plan the next one. And the insights we come up with together get clearer every week, as I get to know you.

props.coachLine: Here's your plan. Your check-in, your reflection, and the habits you picked. Want to start here, or change anything first?

meta.spokenContent: Here's your plan. Your check-in, your reflection, and the habits you picked. Want to start here, or change anything first?

mp3Assets.label: onboard_complete_1
mp3Assets.transcript: Here's your plan. Your check-in, your reflection, and the habits you picked. Want to start here, or change anything first?

props.coachLine: This is your week. Blank, starting today.

meta.spokenContent: This is your week. Blank, starting today.

meta.spokenContent: Most likely your week looks somewhere around here. Mostly green, a few misses. Still a real win.

mp3Assets.label: onboard_weekly_projection_p78_1

props.coachLine: Some weeks can look like this. And even that's okay, because you're in the process and you're consistent inside the process.

props.coachLine: The one thing you want to avoid is this. The empty days you never reported. Stay consistent, just report it. Even a miss counts. That keeps the momentum going.
